questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the past simple correctly with an irregular verb?

options.text: She goed to the market yesterday.
She went to the market yesterday.
She has went to the market yesterday.
She was go to the market yesterday.

questions.explanation: "Go" is an irregular verb whose past simple form is "went". Time markers like "yesterday" confirm the past simple is needed.

questions.prompt: What is the correct form for a past simple question with a regular verb?
questions.explanation: Past simple questions use Did + subject + base verb. After Did, the main verb stays in its base form (no -ed).

questions.prompt: Which structure correctly describes a background action interrupted by a shorter event in the past?

options.text: I cooked when the phone was ringing.
I was cooking when the phone rang.
I was cooking when the phone was ringing.
I cooked when the phone had rung.

questions.explanation: The past continuous (was cooking) provides the background action; the past simple (rang) describes the shorter event that interrupted it.

questions.prompt: Which adverb is associated with the present perfect, NOT the past simple?
questions.explanation: "Already" is a key adverb of the present perfect (have/has + past participle). "Yesterday", "last year", and "in 2020" all indicate a specific past time, so they go with the past simple.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the present perfect?

options.text: She has visited Rome last summer.

questions.explanation: The present perfect (has + past participle) is used with "never" for a life experience at an unspecified time. A specific time like "last summer" would require the past simple instead.
